items.title: WA's unusual 'Easter eggs' captured in aerial images

children.content: Scott McCook spends his spare time photographing the salt lakes of WA's Wheatbelt, which look uncannily like giant eggs when viewed from the air.

gallery.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

thumbnail.altText: A orange salt lake surrounded by red dirt
Over a dozen orange lakes surrounded by white salt borders sit in farm land.
Composite: A pink lake surrounded by crop land. A red red lake framed by trees and pale farm land.

items.title: All WA regional stations must report fuel prices from May as FuelWatch scheme expands

children.content: The FuelWatch reporting scheme is being expanded to all regional fuel stations in Western Australia, with about 200 additional retailers required to report their fuel prices.

image.altText: Fuel pumps and screens depicting prices for petrol and diesal at a service station.

items.title: Travellers celebrate as major highway reopens after Cyclone Narelle

children.content: Holiday makers took a gamble heading to WA's north hoping a major highway would reopen in time for Easter.

image.altText: Four men play cricket on a road with a boat and caravan parked nearby.

items.title: WA's record for heaviest pumpkin broken

children.content: A pumpkin grown in Albany has beaten the previous WA record for heaviest pumpkin by nearly 60 kilograms.

gallery.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

thumbnail.altText: A pumpkin on the back of a truck
A forklift picking up a pumpkin
A medium shot of two sheep eating pumpkin

items.title: Cyclone Narelle's destructive path across Australia, in pictures and video

children.content: From the very first news reports about Severe Tropical Cyclone Narelle, it was clear this wasn't just another storm. This is the story of how the cyclone impacted the entire span of northern Australia and how communities came together to pick up the pieces.

gallery.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

thumbnail.altText: A man looks downcast as he looks at crops
Palm trees in the foreground with a red sky behind.
People look out at the coast as clouds gather.

items.title: Police launch full-scale search to locate woman missing in popular tourism spot

children.content: A large-scale search is underway for 51-year-old Naomi Marie Duckett, who was last seen at Gap Natural Bridge within Torndirrup National Park just before midday on Thursday.

image.altText: A head shot of a 51-year-old woman with black top and grey eyes.

items.title: Woman charged with murder following investigation into death of 24yo

children.content: The woman, 48, and the deceased were known to each other, according to Homicide Squad detectives.

image.altText: A shot of a grey building with the words 'central law courts' written on the side.

items.title: Desperate Raiders head north to face Knights

children.content: With Canberra riding a three-match losing streak the Raiders will be extra keen as they take on the Knights at a sold out stadium in Newcastle. Follow live.

image.altText: A man runs the ball during a rugby league match

items.title: Does a 'historic' plan to protect WA's largest river go far enough?

children.content: A long-awaited draft plan aimed at protecting one of Western Australia's largest rivers has been released for public consultation, but traditional owners, environmental groups and pastoralists say key changes are needed.

image.altText: A river with treed land on either side.

items.title: World-famous Bibbulmun Track considered for heritage list

children.content: Councils support moves for the Bibbulmun Track to be declared a heritage-listed site, which conservationists hope could protect the track from future mining expansions.

gallery.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track
Albany coastline with wind farm in background
A suspension bridge with two people on it high above the ground in a bushland setting.

thumbnail.altText: Hikers and karri trees on the Bibbulmun Track
Albany coastline with wind farm in background
A suspension bridge with two people on it high above the ground in a bushland setting.

items.title: Queen at 100 is honoured in fashion exhibition as Andrew scandal bites

children.content: Within palace walls the Queen was a godlike figure. As her glorious fashion goes on display to commemorate what would have been her 100th birthday, how would she have responded to the Andrew crisis if alive today?

image.altText: Queen Elizabeth II in a long formal blue gown with bolero jacket and headpiece sitting on a red velvet seat with gold trim

items.title: Stagflation is already happening, a leading economist warns

children.content: With stagflation risks rising, what lessons could the treasurer learn from the past?

image.altText: An empty sign on petrol pumps

items.title: Huge sea turtle rescued after being discovered stranded upside down

children.content: A large sea turtle, estimated to be at least 30 years old and weighing more than 50 kilograms, is recovering in a wildlife sanctuary after being spotted stuck in dunes on a northern NSW beach.

image.altText: A large turtle on the sand at a beach.

items.title: Too young for social media, but old enough to be locked up

children.content: As a nation, we have decided that a teenager cannot navigate a social media algorithm, but a 10-year-old can navigate a courtroom. For Australia's 2025 UN youth representative Satara Uthayakumaran, the contradiction is impossible to ignore.

image.altText: A young women sits listening to young unidentified detainees in a youth detention centre

items.title: Artemis crew enter day four with plans to take manual control of spacecraft

children.content: The Artemis astronauts have been taking a moment to look back at Earth as they continue deep into space toward the Moon.

gallery.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.
A round silvery white moon sits in the middle of the picture, surrounded by black space.
A female astronaut is illuminated by a screen inside a darkened spacecraft.

thumbnail.altText: A women peers out of a spacecraft cabin window, looking down at Earth. Her long hair floats upwards in the zero gravity.
A round silvery white moon sits in the middle of the picture, surrounded by black space.
A female astronaut is illuminated by a screen inside a darkened spacecraft.

items.title: Man in serious condition after being shot by Tasmania Police

children.content: A man is in a serious but stable condition after being shot by police during an altercation in Hobart's northern suburbs early on Sunday morning.

image.altText: Two police cars parked on a street with a drone in the air

items.title: Police look for missing cocaine after ocean 'drop-off' during 'bizarre' journey

children.content: Setting off from Panama just before Christmas, police say the MV Raider started its journey towards Sydney with almost 6 tonnes of cocaine aboard worth nearly $2 billion.

image.altText: A helicoper flies over a ship carrying drugs

items.title: Magnitude-5.5 earthquake 'morning wake-up call' to remote community

children.content: A remote community in the APY Lands have woken up to rattling shelves and swinging lights caused by a magnitude-5.5 earthquake, Geoscience Australia says.

image.altText: A red dirt football oval next to a grassed football oval and a small community with a mountain range in the background

items.title: After NT spent 'ridiculous' millions on aircraft, operations move offshore

children.content: A modern version of a 1960s-era aircraft that can land on sea and snow was meant to be built in NSW, and then in the NT. But years later it still hasn’t taken off.

image.altText: A blue plane over the map of the Australia, and images  of Darwin City

items.title: Whole villages in Sumatra erased overnight — and still living in the wreckage

children.content: Months after floods killed more than a thousand people across Sumatra, residents are still digging their way out of the wreckage. While survivors are eager to rebuild, many just can’t shake the fear that the next catastrophe is just around the corner.

gallery.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

thumbnail.altText: A lone house stands in the middle of debris and logs in Sawang district.
Drone footage showing seas of timber throughout Geudumbak — an area east of Sawang district in North Aceh.
A sea of timber running throughout North Aceh after the floods.

items.title: Dirt bike rider, 14, among three dead in separate WA Easter long weekend crashes

children.content: The long weekend is off to a tragic start after two motorcyclists die in separate crashes near Perth, while a woman, 18, is killed in a four-wheel drive rollover in WA's north.

image.altText: A door of a police car with police tape tied to the side mirror.

items.title: Why local fish may be hard to find this Easter in WA

children.content: This Easter is the first in Western Australia under tough new demersal fishing rules. Seafood retailers say the change in supply and price is noticeable.

image.altText: a person purchasing fish from a van

items.title: 'Could get worse': WA Easter holiday-makers anxious amid fuel crisis

children.content: Accommodation providers in regional WA tourism hotspots see a high number of cancellations, as holiday-makers choose to stay close to home due to fuel prices.

image.altText: Large waves crash onto a rocky headland.

items.title: I don't have all day to watch you struggle at an internet quiz

children.content: It's painfully obvious you're going to fail this quiz, so let's get it over with and return to our Sunday mornings, shall we?

image.altText: Tom Gleeson standing with his arms crossed with a serious look on his face with th Hard quiz logo in the top left corner

items.title: Hospitality operators call for end to Rottnest's staffing advantage

children.content: Visa rules allow backpackers to conduct "remote work" on the island, just 33km from Perth's city centre, but not in WA's South West — something business owners want to change.

gallery.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

thumbnail.altText: Two girls at a coffee shop

items.title: New research shows regional Australians move on foot more in 'walkable' towns

children.content: The humble footpath could be the answer to being more active, with new research showing regional Australians living in "walkable towns" travelled 75 minutes more on foot each week.

image.altText: Two people walking along a path near the beach in Warrnambool.

items.title: Thom Yorke's solo tour was one of a kind. This Aussie film captured it

children.content: The award-winning Australian team behind a new concert film of the Radiohead frontman's debut solo tour discusses how it happened and why it matters.

image.altText: Thom Yorke reaches for a knob on a raised piece of equipment, performing live on stage, bathed in blue light

items.title: It hosted Kidman and Luhrmann, now famed regional cinema is up for sale

children.content: It's the cinema good enough for Luhrmann, Jackman and Kidman, and now this North Queensland icon is up for sale after 64 years.

image.altText: An elderly man in a blue tropical printed shirt standing in front of a movie theatre popcorn.

items.title: Your pocket guide to daylight saving time ending in Australia

children.content: The air is getting cooler and the days are about to get shorter as daylight savings comes to an end this weekend.

image.altText: A woman reaches out to an alarm clock from bed.
